---
title: "FHIR → FlatQuack → DuckDB → R"
subtitle: "A synthetic health data analytics showcase"
author: "Fenix / DSG"
date: today
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    self-contained: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r setup}
#| include: false
.libPaths(c("C:/Users/t.hetterscheid/R/library", .libPaths()))

library(DBI)
library(duckdb)
library(dplyr)
library(dbplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(gt)
library(scales)
library(crosstalk)
library(plotly)
library(DT)

DB_PATH <- file.path(dirname(knitr::current_input(dir = TRUE)), "output", "fhir.duckdb")
con     <- dbConnect(duckdb(), dbdir = DB_PATH, read_only = TRUE)
tbl_fhir <- function(name) tbl(con, name)

current_year <- as.integer(format(Sys.Date(), "%Y"))
```

## Overview

This document demonstrates a full healthcare analytics pipeline:

| Step | Tool | What it does |
|------|------|-------------|
| 1 | **FHIR NDJSON** | Synthetic patient data in bulk-export format |
| 2 | **FlatQuack** | Compiles SQL-on-FHIR ViewDefinitions → DuckDB SQL |
| 3 | **DuckDB** | Executes the SQL, creates flat analytical tables |
| 4 | **R + dplyr** | Queries the tables via familiar grammar |
| 5 | **Quarto + OJS** | Renders this interactive report |

The test dataset covers **5 synthetic German patients** with diagnoses, lab results, encounters, and medications.

---

## 1. Patient Cohort

```{r patients}
patients <- tbl_fhir("patient_flat") |>
  select(id, given_name, family_name, gender, birth_date, city) |>
  collect() |>
  mutate(
    full_name = paste(given_name, family_name),
    age       = current_year - as.integer(substr(birth_date, 1, 4))
  )

patients |>
  select(id, full_name, gender, age, city) |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(title = "Patient Demographics") |>
  cols_label(id = "ID", full_name = "Name", gender = "Gender",
             age = "Age", city = "City") |>
  tab_style(style = cell_fill(color = "#f0f4ff"),
            locations = cells_column_labels())
```

```{r age-gender-plot}
#| fig-cap: "Age and gender distribution of the synthetic cohort"
ggplot(patients, aes(x = reorder(full_name, age), y = age, fill = gender)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = age), hjust = -0.2, size = 3.5) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("female" = "#e88dac", "male" = "#6baed6")) +
  labs(title = "Patient Ages by Gender", x = NULL, y = "Age (years)", fill = "Gender") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  ylim(0, max(patients$age) * 1.15)
```

---

## 2. Laboratory & Vital Signs

```{r observations}
observations <- tbl_fhir("observation_flat") |>
  mutate(patient_id = regexp_replace(patient_ref, "Patient/", "")) |>
  left_join(tbl_fhir("patient_flat") |> select(id, family_name, given_name),
            by = c("patient_id" = "id")) |>
  select(patient_id, family_name, given_name, display, category,
         value, unit, interpretation, effective_date) |>
  collect() |>
  mutate(
    date        = as.Date(substr(effective_date, 1, 10)),
    full_name   = paste(given_name, family_name),
    flag        = case_when(interpretation == "H" ~ "High",
                            interpretation == "L" ~ "Low",
                            TRUE                  ~ "Normal")
  )

observations |>
  select(full_name, display, value, unit, flag, date) |>
  arrange(full_name, display) |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(title = "Observations Summary") |>
  cols_label(full_name = "Patient", display = "Measurement",
             value = "Value", unit = "Unit", flag = "Status", date = "Date") |>
  tab_style(style = list(cell_fill(color = "#fff0f0"), cell_text(weight = "bold")),
            locations = cells_body(columns = flag, rows = flag == "High"))
```

```{r obs-plot}
#| fig-cap: "Lab values coloured by interpretation flag"
ggplot(observations, aes(x = display, y = value, colour = flag, shape = full_name)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.85) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("High" = "#d73027", "Normal" = "#1a9850", "Low" = "#4575b4")) +
  labs(title = "Observation Values by Measurement Type",
       x = NULL, y = "Value", colour = "Interpretation", shape = "Patient") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1))
```

---

## 3. Diagnoses — Option A: Multiple Code Systems per Column

A single FHIR `Condition` can carry codes from several terminologies simultaneously.
**Option A** extracts each system into its own column — one row per condition, no row explosion.

The ViewDefinition uses `forEach` on a filtered coding array:

```json
{ "forEach": "code.coding.where(system = 'http://snomed.info/sct')",
  "column": [
    { "name": "snomed_code",    "path": "code" },
    { "name": "snomed_display", "path": "display" }
  ]
},
{ "forEach": "code.coding.where(system = 'http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10')",
  "column": [
    { "name": "icd10_code",    "path": "code" },
    { "name": "icd10_display", "path": "display" }
  ]
}
```

FlatQuack compiles this to `CROSS JOIN UNNEST(list_filter(...))` — keeping one row per condition as long as each system appears exactly once.

```{r conditions}
conditions <- tbl_fhir("condition_flat") |>
  filter(clinical_status == "active") |>
  mutate(patient_id = regexp_replace(patient_ref, "Patient/", "")) |>
  left_join(tbl_fhir("patient_flat") |> select(id, given_name, family_name),
            by = c("patient_id" = "id")) |>
  select(given_name, family_name, condition_display, severity,
         snomed_code, snomed_display, icd10_code, icd10_display, onset_date) |>
  collect()

conditions |>
  mutate(patient = paste(given_name, family_name)) |>
  select(patient, condition_display, severity, snomed_code, icd10_code, onset_date) |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(
    title    = "Active Diagnoses — Option A",
    subtitle = "SNOMED CT and ICD-10 codes extracted as separate columns from the same Condition resource"
  ) |>
  cols_label(patient           = "Patient",
             condition_display = "Condition",
             severity          = "Severity",
             snomed_code       = "SNOMED CT",
             icd10_code        = "ICD-10",
             onset_date        = "Onset") |>
  tab_style(style = cell_fill(color = "#f0fff0"),
            locations = cells_body(columns = snomed_code)) |>
  tab_style(style = cell_fill(color = "#f0f4ff"),
            locations = cells_body(columns = icd10_code)) |>
  tab_style(style = cell_fill(color = "#fff5e6"),
            locations = cells_body(rows = severity == "Moderate"))
```

---

## 4. Medications

```{r medications}
medications <- tbl_fhir("medication_request_flat") |>
  filter(status == "active") |>
  mutate(patient_id = regexp_replace(patient_ref, "Patient/", "")) |>
  left_join(tbl_fhir("patient_flat") |> select(id, family_name),
            by = c("patient_id" = "id")) |>
  select(family_name, medication_display, dosage_text, authored_on, requester) |>
  arrange(family_name) |>
  collect() |>
  mutate(medication_display = gsub('^"|"$', "", medication_display))

medications |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(title = "Active Medication Orders") |>
  cols_label(family_name = "Patient", medication_display = "Medication",
             dosage_text = "Dosage Instructions", authored_on = "Prescribed",
             requester   = "Prescriber") |>
  cols_width(dosage_text ~ px(230))
```

---

## 5. Interactive Explorer {#interactive}

Filter by **patient**, **category**, and **interpretation flag** — all controls are linked:
the dot-plot, the observation table, and the conditions table all update together.
Everything runs client-side via `crosstalk` + `plotly` + `DT`, so the file works when
opened directly from disk (no server, no Shiny).

```{r interactive}
#| echo: false
# ── Shared data objects ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
obs_shared <- observations |>
  transmute(
    Patient   = full_name,
    Category  = category,
    Flag      = flag,
    Test      = display,
    Value     = round(value, 1),
    Unit      = unit,
    Date      = as.character(date)
  ) |>
  SharedData$new(key = ~Patient, group = "fhir")

cond_shared <- conditions |>
  mutate(Patient = paste(given_name, family_name)) |>
  transmute(
    Patient, Condition = condition_display,
    Severity = severity, SNOMED = snomed_code, ICD10 = icd10_code,
    Onset = onset_date
  ) |>
  SharedData$new(key = ~Patient, group = "fhir")

# ── Filter controls ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
filters <- bscols(
  widths = c(4, 4, 4),
  filter_checkbox("patient_flt", "Patient",      obs_shared, ~Patient,  inline = FALSE),
  filter_checkbox("cat_flt",     "Category",     obs_shared, ~Category, inline = FALSE),
  filter_select(  "flag_flt",    "Interpretation",obs_shared, ~Flag)
)

# ── Dot plot (plotly) — tooltip shows patient + value ────────────────────────
dot_plot <- plot_ly(
  obs_shared,
  x       = ~Value,
  y       = ~Test,
  color   = ~Flag,
  colors  = c("High" = "#d73027", "Normal" = "#1a9850", "Low" = "#4575b4"),
  type    = "scatter",
  mode    = "markers",
  marker  = list(size = 12, opacity = 0.85),
  text    = ~paste0("<b>", Patient, "</b><br>", Value, " ", Unit, "<br>", Date),
  hoverinfo = "text"
) |>
  layout(
    xaxis  = list(title = "Value"),
    yaxis  = list(title = ""),
    legend = list(title = list(text = "Flag")),
    margin = list(l = 160)
  ) |>
  highlight(on = "plotly_click", off = "plotly_doubleclick")

# ── Observation table (DT) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
obs_table <- datatable(
  obs_shared,
  colnames    = c("Patient", "Category", "Flag", "Measurement", "Value", "Unit", "Date"),
  rownames    = FALSE,
  options     = list(pageLength = 8, dom = "tip"),
  class       = "compact stripe"
)

# ── Conditions table (DT) — linked by Patient key ────────────────────────────
cond_table <- datatable(
  cond_shared,
  colnames    = c("Patient", "Condition", "Severity", "SNOMED CT", "ICD-10", "Onset"),
  rownames    = FALSE,
  options     = list(pageLength = 8, dom = "tip"),
  class       = "compact stripe"
)

# ── Layout ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
htmltools::tagList(
  htmltools::h4("Controls"),
  filters,
  htmltools::hr(),
  htmltools::h4("Observations — dot plot"),
  dot_plot,
  htmltools::hr(),
  htmltools::h4("Observations — table"),
  obs_table,
  htmltools::hr(),
  htmltools::h4("Diagnoses for selected patients"),
  cond_table
)
```

---

## 6. Cross-Resource Query

> Which diabetic patients have an HbA1c result, and what was it?

```{r cross-query}
diabetic_ids <- tbl_fhir("condition_flat") |>
  filter(condition_display %LIKE% "%Diabetes%") |>
  mutate(patient_id = regexp_replace(patient_ref, "Patient/", "")) |>
  select(patient_id) |>
  distinct()

inner_join(
  diabetic_ids,
  tbl_fhir("observation_flat") |>
    filter(loinc_code == "4548-4") |>
    mutate(patient_id = regexp_replace(patient_ref, "Patient/", "")) |>
    select(patient_id, display, value, unit, effective_date),
  by = "patient_id"
) |>
  left_join(tbl_fhir("patient_flat") |> select(id, given_name, family_name),
            by = c("patient_id" = "id")) |>
  collect() |>
  mutate(date = as.Date(substr(effective_date, 1, 10))) |>
  select(given_name, family_name, display, value, unit, date) |>
  gt() |>
  tab_header(title    = "Diabetic Patients — HbA1c Results",
             subtitle = "HbA1c > 7% indicates sub-optimal glycaemic control") |>
  cols_label(given_name = "Given", family_name = "Family", display = "Test",
             value = "HbA1c (%)", unit = "Unit", date = "Date") |>
  tab_style(style = cell_fill(color = "#ffcccc"),
            locations = cells_body(rows = value > 7))
```

---

## 7. Scopieën met voorafgaande calprotectinemeting

Welk aandeel van de **scopieën** (NZA-codes 034620 / 034686 / 034690 / 035582) heeft een
**calprotectinemeting** (LOINC 38445-3) gedaan in de N dagen ervóór?

De R-chunk leest de scopieën en metingen uit de FHIR DuckDB, berekent voor elk venster
van 1–90 dagen het aantal en percentage, en geeft dat door aan het OJS-dashboard.

```{r scopie-window-prep}
#| include: false
library(purrr)

NZA_CODES    <- c("034620", "034686", "034690", "035582")
CALP_LOINC   <- "38445-3"
MAX_VENSTER  <- 90L

# ── Scopieën uit procedure_flat ───────────────────────────────────────────────
scopieen <- tbl_fhir("procedure_flat") |>
  filter(nza_code %in% NZA_CODES, status == "completed") |>
  select(patient_ref, performed_date) |>
  collect() |>
  mutate(scope_date = as.Date(gsub('^"|"$', "", performed_date)))

# ── Calprotectinemetingen uit observation_flat ────────────────────────────────
calprotect <- tbl_fhir("observation_flat") |>
  filter(loinc_code == CALP_LOINC) |>
  select(patient_ref, effective_date, value) |>
  collect() |>
  mutate(meting_datum = as.Date(substr(effective_date, 1, 10)))

# ── Pre-compute voor elk venster 1–MAX_VENSTER dagen ─────────────────────────
gekoppeld <- inner_join(scopieen, calprotect, by = "patient_ref",
                        relationship = "many-to-many")

window_tbl <- map_dfr(seq_len(MAX_VENSTER), \(n) {
  n_met <- gekoppeld |>
    filter(meting_datum >= scope_date - n, meting_datum < scope_date) |>
    distinct(patient_ref, scope_date) |>
    nrow()

  tibble(
    venster_dagen = n,
    n_met_meting  = n_met,
    n_totaal      = nrow(scopieen),
    pct           = round(n_met / nrow(scopieen) * 100, 1)
  )
})

ojs_define(window_data = window_tbl)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
data = transpose(window_data)

viewof venster = Inputs.range([1, 90], {
  value: 90,
  step: 1,
  label: "Tijdvenster vóór scopie (dagen)"
})

sel = data.find(d => d.venster_dagen === venster)

md`Bij een venster van **${venster} dagen** hebben **${sel.n_met_meting}** van de \
**${sel.n_totaal}** scopieën **(${sel.pct}%)** een calprotectinemeting in de voorafgaande periode.`

Plot.plot({
  width: 680,
  height: 340,
  marginLeft: 60,
  x: { label: "Tijdvenster (dagen vóór scopie)", domain: [1, 90] },
  y: {
    label: "Scopieën met calprotectinemeting",
    grid: true,
    domain: [0, data[data.length - 1].n_totaal + 1]
  },
  marks: [
    Plot.line(data, {
      x: "venster_dagen",
      y: "n_met_meting",
      stroke: "#2166ac",
      strokeWidth: 2.5,
      curve: "step-after"
    }),
    Plot.ruleX([venster], { stroke: "#aaa", strokeDasharray: "5,4" }),
    Plot.ruleY([sel.n_met_meting], {
      x1: 1, x2: venster,
      stroke: "#aaa",
      strokeDasharray: "5,4"
    }),
    Plot.dot([sel], {
      x: "venster_dagen",
      y: "n_met_meting",
      fill: "#d73027",
      r: 7
    }),
    Plot.text([sel], {
      x: "venster_dagen",
      y: "n_met_meting",
      text: d => `${d.n_met_meting}/${d.n_totaal} (${d.pct}%)`,
      dx: 10,
      dy: -8,
      fill: "#d73027",
      fontWeight: "bold",
      fontSize: 13
    })
  ]
})
```

---

## Pipeline Architecture

```
FHIR NDJSON
  Patient · Observation · Condition · Encounter · MedicationRequest
        │
        ▼  *.vd.json  (SQL-on-FHIR ViewDefinitions)
           forEach + where(system=...) → Option A dual-code columns
        │
        ▼  flatquack --mode build
           *.vd.sql  (DuckDB SQL with FHIRPath → struct traversal)
        │
        ▼  setup_db.R
           output/fhir.duckdb
             ├── patient_flat           (id, gender, birth_date, city …)
             ├── observation_flat       (loinc_code, value, unit, flag …)
             ├── condition_flat         (snomed_code, icd10_code …)  ← Option A
             ├── encounter_flat
             └── medication_request_flat
        │
        ▼  showcase.qmd
           Static tables + ggplot2 + OJS interactive explorer
```

```{r teardown}
#| include: false
dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE)
```
